description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane painted in a blue and white livery with the branding 'LAN CHILE' prominently displayed along the fuselage. The aircraft features a streamlined fuselage, swept-back low-mounted wings with visible winglets, and four underwing turbofan engines. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is deployed, showing a tricycle configuration with two main gear units and a nose gear. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows indicating a multi-seat, enclosed design.
description: A large blue and white LAN CHILE commercial airplane with swept low wings, four underwing engines, and a conventional tail. The aircraft has a streamlined fuselage, visible landing gear, and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four engines, and the blue upper fuselage with 'LAN CHILE' branding.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the length of the fuselage, engine placement, and the LAN CHILE logo along the side.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the undercarriage, landing gear, and the arrangement of the engines beneath the wings.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to view 1, showing the opposite side of the fuselage and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
